content: Management begins with medical nutrition therapy: carbohydrate distribution throughout day (minimum 175g/day), focus on low glycemic index foods, avoid excessive weight gain. Self-monitoring of blood glucose: fasting and 1-hour or 2-hour postprandial targets (fasting <95 mg/dL, 1-hour <140 mg/dL, 2-hour <120 mg/dL). If lifestyle modification fails to achieve targets after 1-2 weeks, pharmacotherapy initiated. Insulin is first-line: basal insulin (NPH or detemir) for fasting hyperglycemia; rapid-acting (lispro, aspart) for postprandial elevation. Metformin increasingly used as alternative but crosses placenta; glyburide less favored due to higher neonatal hypoglycemia risk. Postpartum: discontinue medications, screen for persistent diabetes at 6-12 weeks with 75g OGTT, then every 1-3 years.
